description: India Book (IB) II, 65. Letter from an unknown sender in Aden to Abū Yaʿqūb b. Hibat Allah (?) in Lawāmanda (?), possibly an island off the west coast of Burma. "Our nagīd David" mentioned in this letter is probably a descendant of Ḥalfon b. Maḍmūn. The recipient's interests were apparently protected by this 'Nagid David'. The letter reports on the favorable political and economic conditions in Egypt and advises the recipient on the price of commodities such as pepper, two varieties of camphor, nutmeg, and cloves. It also mentions rhubarb, galangal, and aromatic wood. Aden, ca. 1180.
description_he: ספר הודו II,65: מכתב ממשלח לא ידוע בעדן לאבו יעקב בן היבת אללה, על מצב כלכלי ומחירי תבלינים, כ-1180
